item.title: US Stock Futures Extend Rally Ahead of Fed Rate Decision
item.description: US stock futures extend a two-day rally as investors remain cautiously optimistic amid rising energy prices and before the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate decision.

item.title: Japan’s Exports to the US Drop 8%; Takaichi and Trump to Meet Thursday in Washington
item.description: Takaichi faces a diplomatic balancing act after Trump called on Japan to deploy warships to help secure the war-stricken Strait of Hormuz — before abruptly dropping the demand.

item.title: School Bus Unicorn Zum Hits $333 Million in Revenue
item.description: Zum Services Inc., a tech company that provides bus and transit technology to more than 4,000 schools, says it hit $333 million in revenue for 2025, up 35% from the year before.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CF Industries, Coupang, Duolingo, Swarmer
item.description: US equity-index futures pared earlier gains after Iranian state TV said part of the giant South Pars gas field was hit in an airstrike. S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.4% at 7:45 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Chile Economic Growth Beat Forecasts at End-2025 as Mining Rose
item.description: Chile’s economy expanded more than expected in the fourth quarter, helped by growth from the crucial mining sector and expectations for market-friendly government policies under President José Antonio Kast.

item.title: Iran War Poses Risk of ‘Major’ Economic Downturn, Singapore President Says
item.description: A prolonged conflict in the Middle East will heighten the risk of a “major economic downturn” and increased inflation, adding a new stress point in financial markets that were already under strain, Singapore’s President Tharman Shanmugaratnam wrote in a Facebook post.

item.title: AI Security Startup Xbow Valued at More Than $1 Billion
item.description: Xbow, a startup that builds AI software to probe applications for security vulnerabilities, has raised a new round of funding that values it at more than $1 billion, a sign of investor enthusiasm for using artificial intelligence in cybersecurity.

item.title: US Diesel Squeeze Threatens to Ripple Through Economy
item.description: Prices for the fuel have surged faster than most other petroleum-based products.

item.title: Investors Are Wondering If Stocks Have Seen the Worst of the War
item.description: It’s been a volatile stretch for US equities as Wall Street tries to wrap its arms around the war in Iran. But with the fighting now in its third week, investors are becoming more sanguine about the stock market as signs emerge that the worst may be over.

item.title: Tencent Says It Faces Fewer US Regulatory Risks Than TikTok
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. said it faces fewer risks than TikTok in the US as the Trump administration investigates potential security risks involving the Chinese gaming leader’s data practices.

item.title: Prediction Markets Get Tangled In the Iran War
item.description: Reporter threatened by Polymarket users who bet on the attacks

item.title: Trump Calls for Emergency Fed Cut While His Economist Says All’s Well
item.description: US central bank officials are widely seen holding rates as they assess the implications of higher gas prices

item.title: Sweden Is Set for Wait-and-See Stance on Rate
item.description: Sweden’s central bank is set to confirm that war-induced inflation risks have derailed any prospect of an imminent interest-rate cut, when its latest decision is revealed this week.

item.title: Global Bonds on Best Streak Since War Started as Oil Swings Ease
item.description: Bonds around the world are rallying this week, recovering from a big hit since US-Israeli strikes on Iran began, as oil prices calm down.

item.title: Agriculture Firm ETG Tests Demand for Bond Amid Market Turmoil
item.description: ETG Group, which operates agricultural businesses across Africa, is sounding out investors for its first international bond sale, testing demand at a tumultuous time for the sector.

item.title: Elbit Says There’s High Demand for Laser Interceptor in Development
item.description: Israel’s defense giant Elbit Systems Ltd. is in the very advanced stages of developing a high-energy laser system that will potentially provide a cost-effective way to counter aerial threats as countries across the Middle East grapple with the high cost of conventional interceptors.

item.title: OpenClaw Is Giving AI Stock Frenzy a Fresh Push in China
item.description: MiniMax, Zhipu soar after Nvidia CEO touts AI agent tool.

item.title: BHP Ships Rare Jimblebar Cargo to India Amid China Dispute
item.description: BHP Group Ltd. is sending a cargo of Jimblebar fines — a type of iron ore that’s restricted by China’s state buyer — to India, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest sign that the world’s biggest miner is diverting shipments to other markets.

item.title: South Africa Sees Little Scope to Ease Fuel-Price Shock
item.description: South Africa’s National Treasury signaled it has little room to mitigate against the impact of a looming fuel-price shock stemming from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: AI Boom Drives US to Build Enough Battery Systems for Domestic Demand
item.description: The US now has the production capacity to supply 100% of its energy-storage systems domestically, a key milestone in the push to boost American battery manufacturing and decrease the nation’s reliance on Chinese imports.

item.title: Japan Union Chief Says China Rare Earth Curbs Hitting Wage Talks
item.description: The head of a Japanese manufacturers’ labor union group said that China’s recent curbs on rare earth exports are squeezing corporate profits, posing fresh challenges for smaller firms as wage negotiations continue.

item.title: Saudi Bank and Developer Start $1.2 Billion Fund For Holy Cities
item.description: Arabian Dyar, a Saudi developer building thousands of properties across the kingdom, and Al Rajhi Capital have agreed to form a 4.5 billion riyals ($1.2 billion) real estate fund to develop new real estate projects in the holy cities of Mecca and Madinah.

item.title: Apple CEO Visits China Amid Growing Pressure on App Store Policy
item.description: Apple Inc. Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook showed up at a company event in China on Wednesday after the company lowered the fees it collects from app developers in the country earlier this month.

item.title: Ares Leads €300 Million Continuation Fund for Bakery Europastry
item.description: Ares Management Corp. is leading a continuation fund for frozen baked goods firm Europastry SA, owned by MCH Private Equity.

item.title: Will I Get My Full Social Security Benefits? Definitely, Maybe
item.description: Relatively fewer workers are paying in, while retirees are living longer. Something has to change.

item.title: India’s ReNew Energy to Secure $800 Million Refinancing Loan
item.description: Renew Energy Global Plc is set to secure an about $800 million foreign-currency loan, according to people familiar with the matter, in what would be India’s largest offshore financing so far this year.

item.title: Goldman Expects Continued Rebound in Market for European IPOs
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. sees a double-digit pipeline of potential initial public offerings in Europe this year, even as geopolitical tension in the Middle East overshadows sentiment for now.

item.title: India’s Falling FX Buffer Prompts Calls for Softer Rupee Defense
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s recent defense of the rupee is weighing on the nation’s foreign exchange reserves, prompting calls from some analysts to scale back future intervention.

item.title: Brazil’s Long-Awaited Interest Rate Cuts to Start With a Whimper
item.description: Brazil’s central bank is widely expected to begin easing monetary policy with a modest interest-rate cut, as war in the Middle East stokes inflation fears and complicates President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva’s reelection bid.

item.title: Micron Earnings Face High Expectations as Its Stock Keeps Rising
item.description: Soaring prices for memory chips have made Micron Technology Inc. one of the standout stocks of 2026, and its earnings after the close on Wednesday will give clues on whether the rally is sustainable.
